meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Scale SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop hitting scaling walls. Master AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-quality, professional SaaS projects at record speed.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing bugs. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for CLI-based autonomous resolution, you transform debugging from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage. This systematic approach allows you to identify root causes in seconds, drastically reducing cycle times and allowing you to scale your freelance output.
content.intro:
The ceiling is real. You’ve reached the point where your codebase is a labyrinth, your client’s feature requests are piling up, and every 'quick fix' triggers a cascade of regressions. You are effectively trapped by your own success, spending 80% of your billable hours patching holes instead of building the next high-margin module.

This isn't a failure of your skill; it’s a failure of your stack. The modern AI builder doesn't hunt for bugs—they orchestrate agents to find and cauterize them. By embedding AI directly into your terminal and architectural planning, you shift from manual debugger to high-velocity architect, turning complexity into a predictable, scalable stream of deliverables.

steps.title: Map the Dependency Graph
steps.description: Before touching code, use Antigravity to visualize the impact of the bug across your service architecture to avoid unintended side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and generate a dependency graph focused on the authentication module. Highlight potential circular dependencies and high-coupling nodes that could be causing the reported timeout errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze project architecture and create a summary of high-coupling modules'

steps.title: Automate Traceback Analysis
steps.description: Pipe your error logs directly into Claude Code to identify patterns, rather than reading raw stack traces manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Ingest the provided error log and cross-reference it with the codebase to locate the specific function responsible for the memory leak.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Create a sandboxed reproduction script to confirm the bug without affecting the production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a minimal reproduction test case for the isolated bug identified in the previous step using the current project's testing framework.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a unit test file that reproduces the specific failure in the auth service'

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Apply a precise patch via Claude Code, ensuring the fix adheres to your existing project coding standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a refactoring strategy for the identified function to resolve the race condition while maintaining backward compatibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'refactor the target function to handle concurrent requests using the proposed race condition fix'

steps.title: Verify Through Regression
steps.description: Run the full test suite to ensure the fix holds and doesn't break dependent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the test results and confirm that all edge cases related to the bug have been successfully covered by the new test suite.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm test -- --coverage' and verify no regressions in dependent services

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'debug-branch' when running Claude Code to keep your main branch pristine.
Feed your coding style guide into the Claude Code context window to ensure the AI's patches don't look like 'generated code'.
Use Antigravity to visualize 'hot paths' in your code; bugs usually hide where the most traffic flows.
Commit after every successful Claude Code refactor; if the AI suggests a complex change, break it into smaller, atomic commits.
Configure your local environment variables in Claude Code's .env.template so it can simulate integration tests without needing live API keys.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Devs: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-code
content.next_steps: Integrate these tools into your CI/CD pipeline so that every pull request triggers an automated architectural review via Antigravity before reaching your desk.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. With the right AI-driven workflow, you can handle 3x the project load without burning out. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
